commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of guarded-transport compatibility risk, not a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: No supply-chain or secret-handling regression is visible; the diff adds guarded transport wiring and tests without dependency, workflow, lockfile, or credential changes. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` to the OpenAI Responses SDK client and adds constructor-wiring plus real-SDK fetch-path tests.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +163. Total +165 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main lacks the fetch hook on the OpenAI Responses client while OpenAI SDK 6.39.1 uses the configured client `fetch` for Responses requests. I did not run a live oversized-stream reproduction because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of guarded-transport compatibility risk, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(openai-responses): bound SSE response reads via buildGuardedModelFetch
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The OpenAI Responses provider creates an OpenAI SDK client without a custom `fetch` option, so all streaming responses from the Responses API are read without guarded provider transport. A buggy or hostile endpoint returning an oversized body is fully buffered into memory — an OOM / DoS vector.

This injects `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` as the SDK `fetch` option, matching the existing pattern used by the completions path (#97228) and the transport-stream path. The shared guarded fetch pipeline provides SSRF guard, timeout enforcement, retry limiting, and — for non-OpenAI endpoints — 16 MiB body caps via `readResponseWithLimit` and 64 KiB per-event SSE buffer caps.

## Design Rationale

**Why does the default `api.openai.com` endpoint not receive SSE sanitization?** The shared `shouldSanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` predicate (unchanged by this PR) returns `false` for `provider: "openai"` when the base URL hostname is `api.openai.com`. This means the 64 KiB per-event SSE buffer cap and the 16 MiB cumulative JSON-synthesis cap are not applied to the official OpenAI Responses path — matching the behavior of sibling #97228 (openai-completions). The guarded provider transport (SSRF guard, timeout, retry, body-cap on provider JSON responses via `readResponseWithLimit`) applies to all endpoints including `api.openai.com`. A follow-up PR can decide whether to unify the sanitizer predicate.

**Why a separate PR from openai-completions (#97228)?** Each API surface (Responses vs Completions) uses a different SDK client constructor path. Separating the PRs keeps each independently revertible and reviewable, following the campaign pattern: one PR per SDK client.

## Evidence

**Layer 1 — Mock-style constructor-wiring test** (mirrors #97228 pattern):

```
$ pnpm test src/llm/providers/openai-responses.test.ts --run

 RUN  v4.1.8

 ✓ OpenAI Responses SDK client › wires buildGuardedModelFetch into the OpenAI SDK fetch option (123ms)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
```

Proves the SDK constructor receives `buildGuardedModelFetch` as the `fetch` option. Without the +2 LoC, the captured options have no `fetch` key.

**Layer 2 — Real HTTP pipeline proof** (no SDK mock, stubs globalThis.fetch):

```
$ pnpm test src/llm/providers/openai-responses.fetch-proof.test.ts --run

 RUN  v4.1.8

 ✓ OpenAI Responses SDK fetch pipeline (real HTTP proof) › routes the SDK HTTP call through buildGuardedModelFetch to globalThis.fetch (3.13s)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
```

This test does NOT mock the OpenAI SDK. It stubs `globalThis.fetch` and lets the real SDK use `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` as its `fetch` option. The intercepted request confirms:

- URL: `https://api.openai.com/v1/responses` (correct endpoint)
- Method: POST
- Body contains `model: "gpt-5.5"`, `stream: true`, and the correct Responses-API message format
- All SDK retries (3 calls on 500) target the same endpoint

This proves the custom fetch from `buildGuardedModelFetch` is actually invoked by the OpenAI SDK for HTTP requests, not just set in the constructor options.

**CI**: `check-test-types` ✅, `Real behavior proof` ✅, `build-artifacts` ✅, `check-lint` ✅.

**Diff stats**: 3 files, +152/-2 (source: +2, original test: +75, new fetch-proof test: +75).

## Real behavior proof

Same guarded-fetch pipeline as #97228 (openai-completions), which merged with real-server loopback proof through `buildGuardedModelFetch`. This PR adds two-layer proof: (1) constructor wiring via mock, and (2) real HTTP pipeline interception via global fetch stub — both on current HEAD.
target.author: wangmiao0668000666

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
